name: Sheyenne Transport LLC
owner_or_parent: independent (sister company to Duenwald Transportation LLC, both Duenwald family-owned)
fleet_size_est: ~5-10 school buses (sister Duenwald fleet has 8 school + 10 charter)
recent_news: USDA Rural Development success story (n.d., ~2022-2024) profiled the Duenwald family transportation business lines; Sheyenne handles Webster-area school routes while Duenwald (Clark, SD) handles charters and Clark-area routes.
fit_notes: Tiny rural SD operator (~$1M rev, 25 emp); too small for direct platform tuck-in and outside Summit's likely metro focus, but part of a Duenwald-family multi-entity bus group (Sheyenne + Duenwald Transportation) — combined could be more interesting as a regional family roll-up candidate if Summit expands into the Dakotas.

name: Jencarli Transportation Systems
owner_or_parent: independent (LLC); manager Paul White noted on LinkedIn; FMCSA contact A. Mantonucci
fleet_size_est: 45 power units / 39 drivers per FMCSA (historical); USDOT inactive
service_types: freight/trucking (interstate, for-hire) — NOT a confirmed school bus operator
recent_news: FMCSA USDOT 1874542 / MC 677152 shows operating status INACTIVE with last annual mileage reported as 0 (April 2009); related entity Jencarli Transportation Logistics LLC at 11-13 Concord St Newark with USDOT 2130910 (1 truck/1 driver). Appears defunct or dormant as a carrier.
fit_notes: NOT a fit — FMCSA records describe Jencarli as an interstate freight/trucking carrier (now inactive), not a school bus contractor; despite Newark address and ~19 employees, no evidence of pupil-transport operations. Likely a data-source mis-classification; exclude as tuck-in target.

name: KIKI School Bus Transportation Corp
service_types: school bus (presumed; name implies school-bus operator)
districts_or_customers: likely Perth Amboy / Middlesex County NJ districts (unconfirmed)
recent_news: No web presence found beyond data-broker listings; no news, no NJSBCA directory hit; not visible on Perth Amboy district vendor lists in 2024 coverage of Perth Amboy school-bus funding cuts. Perth Amboy district has been cutting busing, which pressures small contractors.
fit_notes: Very small Hispanic-owned (Fernandez) Perth Amboy school-bus corp at residential/light-commercial Oak St address; ~$1M rev, 19 emp suggests ~10-15 buses. Fits Summit's NJ tuck-in profile demographically but minimal scale and no confirmed district contracts; would need primary-source diligence (NJDOE contractor codes, district RFPs) before pursuit.

name: Gen Bus Mgmt Inc
owner_or_parent: independent (presumed; HQ at 1400 Hillside Dr is residential, indicating owner-operator)
service_types: school bus (presumed based on data tag; unconfirmed)
districts_or_customers: likely Cherry Hill / Camden County NJ districts (unconfirmed)
recent_news: No web presence, no news, not in NJSBCA online directory results, not found in NJDOE contractor lists pulled. Phone (856) 429-2972 not linked to a public business listing. Cherry Hill Public Schools transportation office is separate (856-489-5851).
fit_notes: Likely a sub-contractor or micro family operator run from a Cherry Hill residence (1400 Hillside Dr is a 4BR home that sold Nov 2024). At ~$942K revenue and 21 employees too small for direct Summit platform tuck-in; could only matter as a route-bolt-on to a larger South Jersey acquisition. Confirm operating status before any outreach.

name: RNG Transport Inc
districts_or_customers: Essex County NJ districts (county code 13; NJDOE contractor code 7138)
recent_news: Listed in NJ Department of Education school-bus contractor codes as contractor 7138 in Essex County (code 13); no M&A, news, or directory profile surfaced. Distinct from RNG Transport Inc (USDOT 2457814) of Midpines CA and the Canadian freight RNG Transport at rngtrans.com.
fit_notes: Confirmed NJ school-bus contractor (Essex County) but micro-scale (~$942K rev / 20 emp / ~10-12 buses) and no identifiable principal or website. Fits Summit's NJ geography (Essex Co. adjacent to existing Durham/National Express footprint) — viable as a small route-acquisition or driver/asset bolt-on rather than a standalone platform deal.
